action.hypothesis: The catastrophic `K=32` routed result suggests the routed advantage does not extend cleanly into the mid-K band, so the highest-value next move is to strengthen the incumbent `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` anchor instead of retrying routing below `K=64`. A small nonzero `AUXK_ALPHA` may improve feature utilization at `K=32` and reduce FVU without changing the winning mid-K architecture.
action.summary: Run one parameter-only cleanup experiment at `K=32` with `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.2e-3` fixed while changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.0` to `0.01`. Success criterion: beat the current `K=32` frontier anchor FVU (`0.08386963605880737`) and determine whether light aux-k pressure improves the non-routed mid-K recipe.
action.self_review: This keeps to one variable after the clear `K=32` routed miss and focuses on a frontier-relevant smaller-K slot. The main risk is that aux pressure will behave as it did in the earlier low-K soft-codebook probes and slightly worsen FVU, but that is still a clean and informative result for settling the mid-K recipe.
notes_to_memory: Round 99 showed a severe regression for `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at `K=32`, so the routed family should not be treated as the default below `K=64` without a new idea.
The most valuable unresolved slot is the `K=32` mid-K anchor, where improving the incumbent plain two-stage soft-codebook recipe is more promising than more routed confirmations.
This round isolates `AUXK_ALPHA` on the incumbent `K=32` recipe to test whether light dead-feature pressure improves utilization without changing architecture or optimizer.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` wins at `K=32`, propagate the same aux setting to `K=24` under the fixed plain soft-codebook recipe.
If it is close but still behind at `K=32`, test the stronger `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` before abandoning aux tuning on this mid-K family.
If it clearly loses at `K=32`, keep `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` as the settled mid-K recipe and switch the next plain-family cleanup round to an LR sweep instead.

result.description: Run one parameter-only cleanup experiment at `K=32` with `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.2e-3` fixed while changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.0` to `0.01`. Success criterion: beat the current `K=32` frontier anchor FVU (`0.08386963605880737`) and determine whether light aux-k pressure improves the non-routed mid-K recipe.
result.self_review: This keeps to one variable after the clear `K=32` routed miss and focuses on a frontier-relevant smaller-K slot. The main risk is that aux pressure will behave as it did in the earlier low-K soft-codebook probes and slightly worsen FVU, but that is still a clean and informative result for settling the mid-K recipe.
